description: 574 §22.F — the atom-facet catalog (Move 3, self-covering). Maps each visual atom to the raw CSS-class signature of the FORK it replaces. `check-atom-fork-ratchet` PROJECTS its detection vocabulary (the union of every atom's `forkClasses`) and its authority-exclusion set (`atoms[].authorityFile` + `ahaDistinct`, plus every `@atom`-marked file, derived) from this catalog. Adding a new atom row + its fork-classes auto-extends the ratchet's coverage — coverage-projects-from-catalog (557 §5.2); the discovery step is the catalog row, not editing the gate. (Note: bare `.dot` is intentionally NOT a fork-class — too generic, decorative dots are legitimate; the status-indicator fork is `.status-dot`.) `ahaDistinct` holds genuinely-distinct chip concepts that are NOT 'a badge with a tone' and would be over-DRYed by forcing them into jf-status-badge (574 §19.6 AHA cut): ProvenanceChip = trust attribution; DispatchSource = operational provenance (transport/executor/initiator — a 2nd provenance concept distinct from ProvenanceChip's contribution-tier); SourceChipsRenderer = numbered source-citation chips. Each is a single distinct concept (no 2nd instance to unify), so a declared exemption — not a forced atom — is the AHA-correct home.
